commit.message:
Add adaptive inscribed-sphere cache to accelerate Inside(p) (#197)

* Add adaptive inscribed-sphere cache to accelerate Inside(p)

Every DistanceToOut(p) call already returns a provably inscribed sphere
B(p, d) where d is the BVH lower-bound safety distance. Cache these spheres
per-thread and use them to short-circuit Inside(p) before the expensive
BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier::Perform call.

Changes:
- Add InscribedSphere{centre, radius} and SphereCacheData structs.
- Seed fInitialSpheres at construction from up to 15 interior candidate
  points (AABB centre, 6 face midpoints, 8 octant centres) using a local
  BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier for inside-check and BVHLowerBoundDistance
  for the radius. Sorted descending by radius.
- Add G4Cache<SphereCacheData> fSphereCache (thread-local, same pattern
  as fClassifierCache). GetOrInitSphereCache() copies fInitialSpheres on
  first access per thread or after a shape update.
- TryInsertSphere(centre, d): accepts a new sphere only if it is not fully
  dominated by an existing entry (dominance check avoids sqrt via squaring),
  and the cache is bounded to kMaxInscribedSpheres=64 entries sorted
  descending by radius.
- Inside(p): after the AABB early-out, scan the sphere cache before calling
  OCCT. A hit returns kInside immediately (~640 flops worst case vs the
  ~88 000 flops of BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier for a 6-face box).
- DistanceToOut(p): feeds TryInsertSphere after computing the result so
  coverage grows automatically during tracking.
- Destructor: clears fSphereCache.Get().spheres alongside the existing
  classifier/intersector reset.

For g4box-box-20x30x40-v1 the AABB centre seed sphere has radius ≈10 mm,
immediately covering 17% of the interior. After a few dozen tracking steps
the cache reaches near-100% hit rate and the BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier is
never called for interior points.

No new correctness risk: every cached sphere radius d satisfies
d ≤ dist_to_surface(centre) by construction, so no false kInside can occur.

perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop + bbox prefilter (#215)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop

Replace the single IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector::Perform call (33.5%
of total instructions in the B4c callgrind profile, 5,591M instructions)
with a custom per-face loop over IntCurvesFace_Intersector objects.

Two sources of overhead are eliminated:

1. NCollection_Sequence heap management (~31% of total): the ShapeIntersector
   accumulates results in a heap-allocated NCollection_Sequence (ClearSeq,
   Find, AppendPoint, malloc/free).  The per-face loop instead tracks the
   closest hit in a stack-allocated scalar.

2. All-face intersection without prefiltering (~2%): per-face intersectors
   are gated behind Bnd_Box::IsOut(gp_Lin), rejecting faces whose AABB the
   ray misses in O(1).

Implementation details:

- IntersectorCache now holds std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IntCurvesFace_Intersector>>
  (one per face, heap-allocated to avoid movability requirements) and
  std::vector<Bnd_Box> expandedBoxes (each face box enlarged by
  IntersectionTolerance() to prevent IsOut false positives for zero-thickness
  planar face AABBs).

- GetOrCreateIntersector() rebuilds both vectors on generation mismatch.

- DistanceToOut no longer needs the fFaceAdaptorIndex hash-map lookup:
  the loop tracks minFaceIdx directly, giving O(1) access to the cached
  BRepAdaptor_Surface for normal computation.

- DistanceToOut normal condition updated to accept TopAbs_IN || TopAbs_ON,
  fixing pre-existing failures for sphere/cylinder seam hit points that
  IntCurvesFace_Intersector correctly classifies as ON (not IN).

commit.message:
Smart mouse mode: defer disable until click above viewport (#328)

In smart mode, mouse capture was disabled whenever the mouse moved above
the viewport — too aggressive, preventing hover/movement above the
prompt without losing capture. The intended trigger is user intent to
interact with terminal content above (e.g. text selection), which only a
click signals.

## Changes

- **`src/app/mod.rs`**: In the smart mode `on_mouse` handler, gate the
`disable()` call on `MouseEventKind::Down(_)`. Events above the viewport
still return `false` (skipped by flyline) but no longer tear down
capture on mere movement.

```rust
if mouse.row < self.last_viewport_top {
    // Only disable on an actual click above the viewport
    if matches!(mouse.kind, MouseEventKind::Down(_)) {
        self.mouse_state.disable("smart mode: click above the viewport");
    }
    self.last_mouse_over_cell = None;
    return false;
}
```

- **`src/settings.rs`** / **`README.md`**: Updated `MouseMode::Smart`
doc and both README occurrences — "mouse moves above the viewport" →
"user clicks above the viewport".

Auto-update `create-anim` help block in README via workflow (#327)

The `update-settings-docs.yml` workflow only updated the main `flyline
--help` block in the README; the `<!--
FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START/END -->` block was left stale.

## Changes

- **`docker/flyline_help.Dockerfile`**: Added a `RUN` step to capture
`flyline create-anim --help` (ANSI-stripped) into
`/flyline_create_anim_help.txt`; exports it from the `scratch` stage
alongside `flyline_help.txt`.

- **`.github/workflows/update-settings-docs.yml`**: Extended the Python
README-update script to also read `flyline_create_anim_help.txt` and
replace the `FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START/END` markers:

```python
create_anim_help_text = pathlib.Path("flyline_create_anim_help.txt").read_text().rstrip("\n")
new_create_anim_block = f"<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START -->\n```\n{create_anim_help_text}\n```\n<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_END -->"
updated = re.sub(
    r"<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START -->.*?<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_END -->",
    new_create_anim_block,
    updated,
    flags=re.DOTALL,
)
```

G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query (#212)

* G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query

TryGetOutwardNormal was constructing BRepAdaptor_Surface(face) on every
call from DistanceToOut(p,v,...).  In the B4c profile this accumulates to
1,682M instructions (10.1% of total) across 246k calls.

FaceBounds already stores a BRepAdaptor_Surface adaptor per face, built
once in ComputeBounds().  Add a primary TryGetOutwardNormal overload that
takes a const BRepAdaptor_Surface& directly (moving the existing body
there, minus the adaptor construction).  Keep the original TopoDS_Face
overload as a one-line shim for the SurfaceNormal path (~84 calls).

In DistanceToOut(p,v,...) look up intersector.Face(minIndex) in
fFaceBoundsCache via TopoDS_Shape::IsSame() (O(1) TShape pointer
compare) and pass the cached adaptor to the new overload.  A fallback
to on-the-fly construction is retained for any face not found in the
cache (should not occur in practice).

Expected impact: ~10% reduction in instruction count for workloads
dominated by DistanceToOut(p,v) normal queries (B4c calorimeter example).

fix: stale JWT causes silent modal lockout — register/login buttons unresponsive

Root cause: users with a JWT from a pre-wipe account would have isLoggedIn()=true,
so showAuthModal() was never called, leaving the modal visible but with no event
listeners. Buttons appeared clickable but did nothing.

  status:401, same as a real 401 — triggers re-login flow in main.js
- main.js init: when token exists, eagerly validate it via fetchState on load;
  on 401/404 immediately call showAuthModal() to set up fresh listeners
- auth.js showAuthModal: always calls clearSession() at start to purge any stale

commit.message:
Fix heredoc delimiter quoting: apply quote-removal before matching closing line (#320)

The flash lexer stores heredoc delimiter words verbatim (e.g. `<<'EOF'`
→ `HereDoc("'EOF'")`), but bash's closing delimiter is the result of
quote removal on that word — just `EOF`. The dparser was comparing raw
closing-line tokens against the unstripped delimiter, so quoted heredocs
never closed.

## Changes

- **`src/dparser.rs`** — adds `strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes(delim)`
implementing POSIX quote-removal:
  - Single-quoted spans: `'FOO'` → `FOO`
- Double-quoted spans with inner backslash handling per POSIX (`$`, `` `
``, `"`, `\`, newline are special; others keep the backslash)
- Unquoted backslash escapes: `\X` → `X`; lone trailing `\` kept
literally
  - Mixed/partial quoting: `E'O'F` → `EOF`
- Applied at the `HereDoc`/`HereDocDash` match arm before pushing to the
heredocs queue

- **`src/command_acceptance.rs`** — adds
`test_here_documents_quoted_delimiter` covering all four quoting forms
(`'…'`, `"…"`, `\`, mixed) and the `<<-` dash variant

- **`src/dparser.rs` tests** — adds
`test_strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes` (unit) and per-form annotation
tests verifying `IsOpening`/`IsClosing` pairing

```bash
# Previously: heredoc never closed, will_bash_accept_buffer returned false
cat <<'EOF'
hello
EOF        # ← closing line didn't match "'EOF'" stored in queue
```

> The shell does not perform parameter and variable expansion, command

   The artifact is uploaded with two paths (reports/ and bench_raw.json),
   so GitHub Actions preserves the reports/ prefix in the artifact.
   Downloading to docs/reports/ caused double-nesting at
   docs/reports/reports/... (404 for point_cloud_viewer.html and
   test-results.md). Downloading to docs/ maps reports/ correctly to
   docs/reports/.

2. Add family field to each callgrind matrix fixture so the
   family/id key format (e.g. direct-primitives/g4box-box-20x30x40-v1)
   matches what generate_bench_report.py uses when looking up callgrind
   links. Previously only the bare id was used as the key, causing
   callgrind_links.get(fixture_id) to always return None.

3. Update callgrind_links JSON key from bare id to family/id format.

refactor(compressor): merge small/medium → flat, rename helpers, fix key_entities scoring

Strategy:
- _compress_none + _compress_file_level → _compress_flat (< 1000 files)
  4 workers for < 100 files, 2 workers for 100-999 files
- _compress_full_pyramid → _compress_pyramid (1000+ files)
- compression_level strings: 'none'/'file_only'/'full_pyramid' → 'flat'/'pyramid'
- _decide_compression_level: 3 cases → 2 cases
- Add _workers_for() to tune parallelism by file count

Helpers:
- _build_file_summaries_no_llm → _extract_file_metadata
- _build_directory_summaries_no_llm → _extract_directory_metadata

_compress_flat now produces full output:
  Step 1: _extract_file_metadata (AST — symbols, deps, line count)
  Step 2: _summarize_file × N via LLM (prose summary)
  Step 3: _summarize_directory × M via LLM (dir rollup)
  Step 4: _generate_repo_summary via LLM (flat aggregation)

key_entities fix:
- _extract_file_metadata: uses score_entities() not positional slice
- _summarize_file: uses score_entities() not positional slice
- Both now surface the highest-scoring entity in each file, not the

Add callgrind-b4c CI job to profile G4OCCTSolid navigation call distribution (#203)

* Add callgrind-b4c CI job to profile G4OCCTSolid navigation call distribution

B4c uses G4OCCTSolid for the absorber and gap shapes (STEP-imported), making
it the ideal vehicle for profiling realistic G4OCCTSolid call patterns across
all five navigation methods: Inside(p), DistanceToIn(p,v), DistanceToOut(p,v),
DistanceToIn(p) [safety], DistanceToOut(p) [safety].

Changes:

src/examples/B4c/CMakeLists.txt:
- Add option(B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS ON): allows CI to pass OFF to prevent placement
  overlap checking from inflating Inside() call counts during construction.
- target_compile_definitions: B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=<1|0> passed to the compiler
  so DetectorConstruction.hh can use it as a default member initializer.
- if(VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR): add HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H + include path for
  exampleB4c, enabling CALLGRIND macros in RunAction.

src/examples/B4c/include/DetectorConstruction.hh:
- fCheckOverlaps default initializer changed from true to B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS
  so the CI profiling job disables it at compile time without touching source.

src/examples/B4c/src/RunAction.cc:
- Add CALLGRIND guard block (HAVE_VALGRIND_CALLGRIND_H) at top.
- BeginOfRunAction: CALLGRIND_START_INSTRUMENTATION + CALLGRIND_TOGGLE_COLLECT
  -> geometry construction and physics init excluded from profile.
- EndOfRunAction:   CALLGRIND_TOGGLE_COLLECT + CALLGRIND_STOP_INSTRUMENTATION.

src/examples/B4c/run_profile.mac (new):
- 100 events, e- 300 MeV, /run/verbose 0.  Enough statistics for stable
  per-method call ratios; with --instr-atstart=no, runtime is manageable.

.github/workflows/ci.yml:
- New callgrind-b4c job: builds exampleB4c with B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF and
  VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR, runs run_profile.mac under callgrind with gated
  instrumentation, annotates with callgrind_annotate, uploads artifact
  callgrind-reports-b4c (annotated txt + raw .out, 7-day retention).
